title: Club de la Obra Publica — Argentine Construction Cartel
summary: Map of Argentine construction companies that systematically divided public works contracts, paid kickbacks to officials, and rotated across administrations from the military dictatorship (1976-83) through Milei (2024-present). The 'Cuadernos de las Coimas' case (2018-present, trial ongoing 2025-26) exposed the mechanism in detail.

cartel_mechanism.name: Club de la Obra Publica / La Camarita
how_it_works: Companies met at the Argentine Chamber of Road Companies (Camara de Empresas Viales) headquarters, known as 'La Camarita'
Before each bidding process, companies were told who would win and what kickbacks to pay
Losing bidders submitted inflated bids to make the winner's price look competitive
The surplus from inflated prices covered: (a) kickbacks to officials (10-20% of contract value), (b) compensatory shares to companies that 'lost' the bid
The Registry of Constructors (Registro de Constructores) under Law 13.064 legally limited who could bid, creating a closed club for 70+ years
The 'capacidad constructiva' (constructive capacity) metric ensured work was distributed among existing members and blocked new entrants
cartel_mechanism.key_confession: Carlos Enrique Wagner, ex-president of the Argentine Chamber of Construction, confessed how companies divided contracts and paid bribes of 10-20% of each project's value
cartel_mechanism.financial_operator: Ernesto Clarens, financier, received cash from companies, converted to USD, delivered to officials (e.g. Jose Lopez), charged commission on exchange spread
cartel_mechanism.reform_2025: Milei/Sturzenegger eliminated the Registro de Constructores and the capacidad constructiva system in May 2025, replaced with SICO (Sistema de Informacion de Cocontratantes). Vialidad Nacional dissolved July 2025 (decree 461/2025).

techint_group.founding: 1945 by Agostino Rocca (Italian, ex-advisor to Mussolini on steel). First major contract: 33,000 tons of seamless pipes for Gas del Estado (1947). 1,600 km gas pipeline Comodoro Rivadavia-Buenos Aires under Peron (1949).

key_subsidiaries.tenaris: Seamless tubes. Listed Luxembourg. Uruguayan shell companies explain 15-22% of gross profits via transfer pricing.
key_subsidiaries.tecpetrol: Oil & gas. Filed USD 96M lawsuit vs ENARSA (2026) + prior USD 640M lawsuit (2019) over Plan Gas subsidies.
key_subsidiaries.siat: Pipe manufacturing. Won Gasoducto NK pipe supply as sole bidder.

offshore_structure.shell_companies: Multiple entities in Uruguay with no productive activity but intense financial/commercial exchange with Argentine operating companies
offshore_structure.profit_shifting: Transfer price manipulation routes profits to Uruguayan shells. Intangible assets (brands, patents developed by Argentine researchers) transferred to offshore structures.

state_benefits_history: Public works contracts (gas pipelines, roads) since 1947
Industrial promotion regime subsidies with tax exemptions
Foreign debt insurance (seguros de cambio) that socialized private debt
Compre Nacional law — State paid premium prices for domestic suppliers
22% of first road concessions (1988, Alfonsin era)

gasoducto_nestor_kirchner.pipe_supply: USD 500M contract (SIAT sole bidder: 582km of 36-inch + 74km of 30-inch pipe). Actual bid: USD 436M.
gasoducto_nestor_kirchner.construction: Techint + Sacde consortium won major construction lots.

techint_group.cuadernos_status: Techint executives implicated. Cartel member since at least 1981 (4 instances in 1982 complaint).
techint_group.milei_era: Sturzenegger accused Techint of offering pipes 40% more expensive than Indian competitor. Techint suing state for USD 96M + USD 640M. Relationship strained.

grupo_roggio.founding: Grew under military dictatorship (1976-83). Built ATC, Chateau Carreras stadium, thermal power plants and airports in Tucuman and Cordoba.

key_holdings.clisa: Compania Latinoamericana de Infraestructura y Servicios. Entered default 2024 due to public works freeze under Milei.
key_holdings.metrovias_emova: Buenos Aires subway concession since 1994 (Menem). Renewed 2020 for 12 years as 'Emova Movilidad'. Currently runs all 6 subway lines + Premetro.
key_holdings.aguas_cordobesas: Water service concession for Cordoba city since 1997

grupo_roggio.state_contracts_volume: Approx ARS 8 billion in state contracts 2003-2012. Clarens list: ARS 2.3 billion in works as of April 2010. 22% of first road concessions (1988).

cuadernos_case.status: Aldo Roggio declared as arrepentido (cooperating witness) in August 2018
cuadernos_case.confession: Admitted paying 5% of transport subsidies (Metrovias, Ferrovias Urquiza) to Kirchner Transport Secretary Ricardo Jaime
cuadernos_case.charges: 11 spurious payments June 2009 - September 2010 totaling ARS 5.9 million. Received 22 works from Vialidad Nacional worth over ARS 3.3 billion.
cuadernos_case.offshore: Used 3 foreign companies to open accounts in Switzerland and USA (revealed in Pandora Papers)

iecsa_sacde.current_name: SACDE (Sociedad Argentina de Construccion y Desarrollo Estrategico)
iecsa_sacde.current_controller: Marcelo Mindlin / Damian Mindlin (via Pampa Energia partners)

ownership_chain.notes: Part of SOCMA holding. 17% of first road concessions (1988).
Bought to avoid conflict of interest when Mauricio Macri became Buenos Aires Mayor. Ranked 3rd largest state contractor under Kirchner (behind Techint and Electroingenieria).
Purchased via MBD-Lazard auction. Renamed to SACDE. Payment dispute: Calcaterra sued Mindlin over unpaid installments (6 annual payments 2019-2024).

iecsa_sacde.key_project: Sarmiento train burial (soterramiento) — won during Kirchner era in consortium with Odebrecht, Ghella, Comsa

cuadernos_case.calcaterra_status: Declared as arrepentido before Judge Bonadio. Admitted payments to Roberto Baratta for 2013/2015 Kirchnerist campaigns.
cuadernos_case.contradictions: Before Judge Martinez de Giorgi (Odebrecht case): denied paying bribes. Before Judge Bonadio (Cuadernos): admitted payments.
cuadernos_case.casacion_ruling_2024: Casacion removed Calcaterra from Cuadernos trial (June 2024), then ruling was challenged. Payments to be judged as possible sobornos (Nov 2024).
cuadernos_case.odebrecht_link: Iecsa consortium partner Odebrecht admitted bribes for Sarmiento burial project

milei_era.contracts: Sacde + Techint consortium won North Gas Pipeline reversal. Sacde contracted for Pampa Energia VI wind park. Competing for highway concessions.
milei_era.mindlin_relationship: Publicly supportive of Milei. Inaugurated USD 260M wind park with government presence (Sept 2024).

electroingenieria.sector: Energy infrastructure, power plants, dams, nuclear
electroingenieria.status_kirchner_era: Favorite firm of Kirchner period. Ranked among top 3 state contractors (behind Techint, alongside IECSA).

pilar_power_plant.irregularity: No public bidding — only bid (2007) was for repowering old plant, abandoned, then awarded to Electroingenieria-Sener UTE in 2008 without new call

cuadernos_case.ferreyra_status: Did NOT become arrepentido. Was imprisoned, later granted house arrest. One of few businessmen who refused to cooperate.
cuadernos_case.ferreyra_position: Claims case was 'fabricated to destroy national businessmen.' Says arrepentidos gave false testimony to remove him from dam project won legitimately.
cuadernos_case.swiss_documents: Documents from Switzerland complicated Electroingenieria in bribery case (2021)
cuadernos_case.supreme_court_2024: Corte Suprema ruled against Ferreyra in Cuadernos case (Nov 2024)

electroingenieria.nuclear_views: Ferreyra advocates state control of energy including nuclear. Notes Argentina's nuclear experience since 1947 (Atucha I, Embalse, Atucha II). Kirchner government resumed nuclear plan.

other_cartel_members.name: Cartellone
other_cartel_members.note: Executives implicated in Cuadernos. Built Vega creek entubement with Roggio and Supercemento (USD 177M, 2016-2020).

other_cartel_members.name: Supercemento
other_cartel_members.note: Executives implicated in Cuadernos. Partner on major Buenos Aires infrastructure.

other_cartel_members.name: Criba
other_cartel_members.note: Owned by Tarasido family. Cambiemos campaign contributor 2015. Buenos Aires contracts exceeding USD 180M.

other_cartel_members.name: Decavial / Losi / Panedile / Coarco / Equimac
other_cartel_members.note: Owners/directors declared as arrepentidos or indicted in Cuadernos. Confessed to paying bribes.

other_cartel_members.name: Vial Agro / Perales Aguiar / Biancalani / Fontana Nicastro / Marcalba / Chediak / Vialco
other_cartel_members.note: All named in La Camarita cartel distribution lists.

other_cartel_members.name: SADE (now Skanska)
other_cartel_members.note: 6 instances in 1982 cartel complaint. International construction firm.

other_cartel_members.name: Impresit Sideco (SOCMA/Macri)
other_cartel_members.note: 3 instances in 1982 cartel complaint. Part of Franco Macri's group.

other_cartel_members.name: Green SA / Dycasa / Conorvial / Rottio / Sudamericana
other_cartel_members.note: Major Buenos Aires public works contractors under Larreta administration.

cuadernos_case.full_name: Cuadernos de las Coimas / Causa Cuadernos
cuadernos_case.origin: Notebooks kept by Oscar Centeno, driver/remisero of Roberto Baratta (Secretary of Energy, Ministry of Planning under Kirchner governments 2003-2015)
cuadernos_case.scope: 71 companies investigated. Covers public works contracts 2003-2015.
cuadernos_case.trial_status: Oral trial began late 2025. Ongoing as of March 2026. Centeno returned to court March 19, 2026 but refused to testify.

political: Cristina Fernandez de Kirchner — principal imputada (main defendant)
Roberto Baratta — Secretary of Energy, recipient of bribes
Jose Lopez — Planning Ministry official, received cash deliveries
Ricardo Jaime — Transport Secretary, received 5% kickbacks on subsidies
Julio De Vido — Planning Minister, oversaw contract distribution
financial_operators: Ernesto Clarens — financier, converted cash to USD, delivered to officials
Oscar Centeno — driver who kept the notebooks documenting deliveries
empresarios_arrepentidos: Carlos Enrique Wagner (Chamber of Construction president) — confessed cartel mechanism
Aldo Roggio — admitted 5% kickbacks on transport subsidies
Angelo Calcaterra — admitted payments to Baratta for campaigns
Multiple executives from Decavial, Supercemento, Losi, Panedile, Coarco, Equimac
empresarios_who_refused_cooperation: Paolo Rocca (Techint) — implicated but did not become arrepentido

cuadernos_case.total_contracts_investigated: Over ARS 3.3 billion in road works alone (Roggio). 71 companies total.
cuadernos_case.restitution_offers: 48 imputados offered restitution payments to avoid oral trial (Sept 2025). Roggio offered USD 1,267,108.

territory_division.description: How major groups historically divided Argentine public works territory

kirchner_era_ranking.note: Per ranking released by Planning Minister Julio De Vido

buenos_aires_city.top_7_absorb: Nearly 40% of total porteño infrastructure investment

milei_era_winners.techint_sacde: Joint consortium for gas infrastructure (North Gas Pipeline reversal, Gasoducto NK)
milei_era_winners.mindlin: First Milei-era public work financed with private funds. Wind parks. Highway concessions.
milei_era_winners.new_entrants: Indian pipe companies (accused by Techint of undercutting). 15 companies competing for route privatizations.

mechanism: 1. Companies receive state contracts (public works, concessions, subsidies)
3. Cash from margin is collected by financial operators (e.g. Clarens)
4. Cash converted to USD, delivered to officials (documented in Centeno's notebooks)
5. Some funds go to electoral campaign financing (admitted by Calcaterra for 2013/2015 campaigns)
6. Companies prohibited from donating to campaigns do so via 'black money' (off-books)
7. Officials who receive funds ensure continued contract flow to same companies
8. Registry of Constructors (until 2025) legally blocked new competitors from entering
money_loop.transport_subsidy_variant: Roggio admitted returning 5% of transport subsidies (Metrovias/Ferrovias) directly to Transport Secretary Ricardo Jaime
money_loop.campaign_finance_law: 2019 reform enabled corporate donations but companies with public works contracts remain prohibited from donating. Per Poder Ciudadano: most campaign money remains 'black' (undeclared).

historical_cartel_complaint_1981.description: First documented complaint about the construction cartel, filed 1981-82
